metadata.summary: A tiny jit vector database with minimal dependencies and maximum performance.

documentation:
## Tiny Vector Database

A lightweight vector database designed for small projects.

**Features**
- Just-in-time (JIT) compiling to optimize vector operations by setting the vector size at compile time.
- Accelerates vector operations using [Eigen](https://eigen.tuxfamily.org/index.php?title=Main_Page).
- Processes vectors using only Python lists, no need for any additional third-party data formats.
- Stores vectors as base-64 encoded strings in a SQLite database.

**Performance**  
More than 10x Faster than numpy-based vector operations.

### Development Status

It is currently compatible with g++ or clang++.   
You may need to modify `compile_config` parameter in `VectorDatabase` initialization to inject your compile commands.   
To make it work with other compiler, you may need to change `tiny_vectordb.jit` module.

### Installation

```bash
pip install tiny_vectordb
```
Good to go!

<details>
<summary> Uninstallation (before version 0.1.11) </summary>

### Uninstallation
Previous to version 0.1.11, the package will emit some compiled files in the source directory, which may not be automatically removed using `pip uninstall`, so you need to run the following command manually if you want to uninstall the package comletely. 
(After 0.1.11, you can also use the following command to clean up the cache files.)
```bash
python -c "import tiny_vectordb; tiny_vectordb.cleanup()"
```
After that, you can safely uninstall the package with: 
```bash
pip uninstall tiny_vectordb
```
</details>

### Usage:
```python
from tiny_vectordb import VectorDatabase

collection_configs = [
    {
        "name": "hello",
        "dimension": 256,
    },
    {
        "name": "world",
        "dimension": 1000,
    }
]
database = VectorDatabase("test.db", collection_configs)
collection = database["hello"]

# add vectors
collection.setBlock(
    ["id1", "id2"],             # ids
    [[1] * 256, [2] * 256]      # vectors
)

# search for nearest vectors
search_ids, search_scores = collection.search([1.9] * 256)  
```
For more usage, see `example.py`.

---

**Designing Note:**  

1. No numpy array is used in the database, because I want it to be as lightweight as possible, and lists of numbers are eaiser to be converted into json for communication with http requests.

2. The data are always stored in contiguous memory to ensure the best searching performance.  
So the addition and deletion are preferred to be done in batches as they envolve memory reallocation.   
Here are some useful functions for batch operations:
```python
class VectorCollection(Generic[NumVar]):
    def addBlock(self, ids: list[str], vectors: list[list[NumVar]]) -> None:
    def setBlock(self, ids: list[str], vectors: list[list[NumVar]]) -> None:
    def deleteBlock(self, ids: list[str]) -> None:
    def getBlock(self, ids: list[str]) -> list[list[NumVar]]:
```

modules.name: tiny_vectordb
documentation:
vecotr related modules.
vector database and related functions.

functions.name: tiny_vectordb.jit_utils.autoCompileConfig
documentation:
Return automatically detected compile config for the current environment. 
Will check existence of compilers and files.
TODO: improve this...

methods.name: tiny_vectordb.numpy_impl.VectorCollection_Numpy.addBlock
documentation: Add a bulk of elements, will raise error if id exists

methods.name: tiny_vectordb.numpy_impl.VectorCollection_Numpy.deleteBlock
documentation: Delete a bulk of elements, will raise error if id not exists

methods.name: tiny_vectordb.numpy_impl.VectorCollection_Numpy.setBlock
documentation:
For every element in ids, set the corresponding vector
Add if not exists, update if exists

methods.name: tiny_vectordb.numpy_impl.VectorCollection_Numpy.update
documentation: Change a vector, will raise error if id not exists

methods.name: tiny_vectordb.numpy_impl.VectorCollection_Numpy.has
documentation: Check if id exists

methods.name: tiny_vectordb.numpy_impl.VectorCollection_Numpy.keys
documentation: Return all ids in this collection

methods.name: tiny_vectordb.numpy_impl.VectorCollection_Numpy.get
documentation: Get a vector by id, will raise error if id not exists

methods.name: tiny_vectordb.numpy_impl.VectorCollection_Numpy.getBlock
documentation: Get a bulk of vectors by ids, will raise error if any id not exists

methods.name: tiny_vectordb.numpy_impl.VectorCollection_Numpy.search
documentation: Search for top-k vectors, return ids and scores

methods.name: tiny_vectordb.numpy_impl.VectorCollection_Numpy.load
documentation: Load a bulk of elements, should be called when the collection is empty

methods.name: tiny_vectordb.numpy_impl.VectorCollection_Numpy.loadFromDisk
documentation:
Load all data of the collection from disk, 
Should be called when the collection is attached to a database and is empty

methods.name: tiny_vectordb.numpy_impl.VectorCollection_Numpy.flush
documentation:
Load all changes to sqlite database memory, but not save to disk,
If the collection is not attached to a database, return False

functions.name: tiny_vectordb.config.cleanup
documentation: Should be called before using `pip uninstall`
